Class TracedOutbox

java.lang.Object
io.github.lobofoltran.outbox.otel.TracedOutbox
All Implemented Interfaces:
Outbox
public final class TracedOutbox extends Object implements Outbox
Decorator that wraps every publish and publishAll call in an OpenTelemetry span using the messaging.* semantic conventions, and injects the active trace context into the event's headers so downstream consumers (relay / CDC / broker) can create a linked CONSUMER span from the event alone.

Spans emitted:

  • outbox publish — single event publish. Span kind SpanKind.PRODUCER. Attributes: messaging.system, messaging.operation=publish, messaging.message.id, messaging.destination.name (when non-null), outbox.aggregate_type, outbox.event_type. messaging.system defaults to outbox (the publisher is broker-agnostic); when OutboxEvent.destination() carries a URI-style scheme prefix such as kafka://orders — discouraged but tolerated — the scheme is lifted into messaging.system (lower-cased) and the remainder (orders) becomes messaging.destination.name. This keeps the attribute aligned with the OpenTelemetry messaging semantic conventions, under which messaging.destination.name is the broker-specific logical destination name with no transport scheme.
  • outbox publish_batch — batch publish. Span kind SpanKind.PRODUCER. Attributes: messaging.system=outbox, messaging.operation=publish_batch, messaging.batch.message_count. Each event in the batch carries its own injected context — the same parent span, but the values written by the propagator are computed per event so a broker that links one consumer span per message remains correct.

By design, neither aggregate_id nor destination appears in the span name (low-cardinality only). The same cardinality rules that govern metric tags apply here.

Trace-context propagation defaults to the W3C traceparent / tracestate format via W3CTraceContextPropagator.getInstance(). Callers needing a different wire format (B3, Jaeger) can pass an explicit TextMapPropagator to the matching constructor — or, when wiring through TracedOutbox(Outbox, OpenTelemetry), the propagator is sourced from OpenTelemetry.getPropagators() so it follows the application's global SDK configuration.

Headers injected by the propagator never overwrite headers the caller already set on the OutboxEvent: caller-supplied keys win. This lets a caller carrying a trace context from another transport (e.g. an inbound HTTP request whose traceparent should be preserved verbatim) pass it through unchanged.

On exception, the span status is set to StatusCode.ERROR and the exception is recorded via Span.recordException(Throwable); the original exception is rethrown unchanged.

Instances are thread-safe: the Tracer and TextMapPropagator contracts permit concurrent use, and the decorator holds no mutable state of its own.

Since:
0.1.0

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, OpenTelemetry otel)
    Convenience constructor that builds a Tracer from otel pinned to the outbox-otel instrumentation scope ("io.github.lobofoltran.outbox") and to this library's published version (read from Package.getImplementationVersion(), falling back to "unknown" when the JAR is exploded on the classpath without a manifest — typically only the case in IDE / reactor builds).
    TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, Tracer tracer)
    Wraps delegate so every publish call emits a span on the supplied Tracer and propagates trace context via the W3C traceparent / tracestate headers.
    TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, Tracer tracer, io.opentelemetry.context.propagation.TextMapPropagator propagator)
    Wraps delegate so every publish call emits a span on the supplied Tracer and propagates trace context using the supplied TextMapPropagator.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    publish(OutboxEvent event)
    Persists the given event to the outbox table.
    void
    publishAll(Iterable<OutboxEvent> events)
    Persists every event in events to the outbox table, atomically with respect to the caller's transaction.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • TracedOutbox

      public TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, Tracer tracer)
      Wraps delegate so every publish call emits a span on the supplied Tracer and propagates trace context via the W3C traceparent / tracestate headers.

      This is the lowest-level constructor; the caller is responsible for obtaining the Tracer from its own TracerProvider, including setting an instrumentation scope name and version that fit its observability conventions. Prefer TracedOutbox(Outbox, OpenTelemetry) when those defaults are acceptable.

      Parameters:
      delegate - the underlying Outbox; never null.
      tracer - the Tracer to record spans on; never null.
      Since:
      0.1.0

    • TracedOutbox

      public TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, Tracer tracer, io.opentelemetry.context.propagation.TextMapPropagator propagator)
      Wraps delegate so every publish call emits a span on the supplied Tracer and propagates trace context using the supplied TextMapPropagator. Use this constructor to opt into B3, Jaeger, or any composite propagator the application has standardized on.
      Parameters:
      delegate - the underlying Outbox; never null.
      tracer - the Tracer to record spans on; never null.
      propagator - the TextMapPropagator used to inject context into event headers; never null.
      Since:
      0.2.0

    • TracedOutbox

      public TracedOutbox(Outbox delegate, OpenTelemetry otel)
      Convenience constructor that builds a Tracer from otel pinned to the outbox-otel instrumentation scope ("io.github.lobofoltran.outbox") and to this library's published version (read from Package.getImplementationVersion(), falling back to "unknown" when the JAR is exploded on the classpath without a manifest — typically only the case in IDE / reactor builds). The TextMapPropagator is taken from OpenTelemetry.getPropagators() so it follows the application's globally configured propagation format.

      The Spring Boot auto-configuration uses this constructor so the instrumentation scope name and version follow the library, not the application. Manual wiring code should prefer this constructor too unless the caller needs a non-default tracer or propagator.

      Parameters:
      delegate - the underlying Outbox; never null.
      otel - the OpenTelemetry used to obtain a Tracer and the propagator; never null.
      Since:
      0.1.0

  • Method Details

    • publish

      public void publish(OutboxEvent event)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists the given event to the outbox table.

      The write is performed inside the caller's active transaction and is rolled back together with the caller's business writes if that transaction aborts.

      Specified by:
      publish in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      event - the event to persist; never null.

    • publishAll

      public void publishAll(Iterable<OutboxEvent> events)
      Description copied from interface: Outbox
      Persists every event in events to the outbox table, atomically with respect to the caller's transaction.

      The default implementation simply iterates and calls Outbox.publish(OutboxEvent) per event. Implementations are encouraged to override this with a true batch write (e.g. JDBC addBatch / executeBatch) when the underlying store supports it. The default exists for source compatibility — every Outbox produced before this method was added still satisfies the contract.

      If any individual write fails, the surrounding transaction will not be committed by this library; the failure surfaces as an OutboxException subtype on the offending event.

      Specified by:
      publishAll in interface Outbox
      Parameters:
      events - the events to persist; never null, individual elements must not be null.
